title: Plastic Tide: How Our Waste Is Choking the Ocean
title_zh: 塑料潮：我们的垃圾如何窒息海洋
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，探讨塑料污染对海洋生态的严重影响，包括微塑料的危害、对野生动物的影响以及个人和社会的解决方案。包含四级核心词汇标注、中文翻译和阅读理解题。

paragraphs.en: Every minute, the equivalent of one garbage truck of plastic enters the ocean. This shocking fact reveals a crisis that is often invisible but deeply impacts marine life and human health. From the surface to the deepest trenches, plastic has become an unwanted part of the ocean ecosystem.
paragraphs.zh: 每分钟，相当于一垃圾车的塑料进入海洋。这一令人震惊的事实揭示了一场通常看不见但深刻影响海洋生物和人类健康的危机。从海面到最深的海沟，塑料已成为海洋生态系统中不受欢迎的一部分。

paragraphs.en: The problem starts with our daily habits. Plastic bags, bottles, and packaging are used for minutes but can persist in the environment for hundreds of years. Sunlight, waves, and heat break them down into tiny particles called microplastics, which are smaller than a grain of rice. These particles are now found everywhere, from Arctic ice to the fish we eat.
paragraphs.zh: 问题始于我们的日常习惯。塑料袋、瓶子和包装被使用几分钟，却能在环境中持续存在数百年。阳光、海浪和热量将它们分解成称为微塑料的微小颗粒，比米粒还小。这些颗粒现在无处不在，从北极冰到我们吃的鱼。

paragraphs.en: Marine animals often mistake plastic for food. Sea turtles confuse plastic bags with jellyfish, their favorite meal. Whales and fish swallow large amounts of plastic, which blocks their digestive systems and causes starvation. A study found that 90% of seabirds have plastic in their stomachs. By 2050, that number could reach 99%.
paragraphs.zh: 海洋动物经常将塑料误认为食物。海龟将塑料袋与水母混淆，而水母是它们最喜欢的食物。鲸鱼和鱼类吞下大量塑料，这会阻塞它们的消化系统并导致饥饿。一项研究发现，90%的海鸟胃里有塑料。到2050年，这一数字可能达到99%。

paragraphs.en: Microplastics pose an even greater threat. They can absorb toxic chemicals from the water, becoming poisonous capsules. When smaller fish eat these particles, the chemicals move up the food chain. Eventually, they end up on our dinner plates. Scientists have found microplastics in human blood, lungs, and even placentas.
paragraphs.zh: 微塑料构成了更大的威胁。它们可以从水中吸收有毒化学物质，成为有毒胶囊。当小鱼吃掉这些颗粒时，化学物质会沿着食物链向上移动。最终，它们出现在我们的餐盘上。科学家在人类血液、肺部甚至胎盘中发现了微塑料。

paragraphs.en: The good news is that solutions exist. Many countries have banned single-use plastics like straws and bags. In 2022, 175 nations agreed to create a global plastic treaty by 2024. Individuals can also make a difference by reducing reliance on plastic and choosing reusable alternatives. Every action counts, no matter how small.
paragraphs.zh: 好消息是存在解决方案。许多国家已经禁止了一次性塑料制品，如吸管和袋子。2022年，175个国家同意在2024年前制定一项全球塑料条约。个人也可以通过减少对塑料的依赖和选择可重复使用的替代品来做出改变。每一个行动都很重要，无论多么微小。

paragraphs.en: Technology also offers hope. Scientists have developed bacteria that can eat plastic, turning it into hydrocarbon fuel. Others have created biodegradable plastics from corn or algae. While these innovations are promising, they are not yet cheap enough to replace traditional plastics on a large scale.
paragraphs.zh: 技术也带来了希望。科学家开发了可以吃塑料的细菌，将其转化为碳氢燃料。其他人用玉米或藻类制造了可生物降解的塑料。虽然这些创新很有前景，但它们的成本还不够低，无法大规模替代传统塑料。

paragraphs.en: Raising awareness is equally important. Documentaries like 'Blue Planet II' have shown the public the grim reality of plastic pollution. Schools and communities now teach children about the 3Rs: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle. With education, the next generation can grow up with a stronger sense of environmental responsibility.
paragraphs.zh: 提高意识同样重要。像《蓝色星球II》这样的纪录片向公众展示了塑料污染的严峻现实。学校和社区现在教孩子们3R原则：减少、再利用、回收。通过教育，下一代可以带着更强的环境责任感成长。

paragraphs.en: The ocean covers 71% of our planet and produces over half of the oxygen we breathe. Protecting it from plastic is not just about saving cute sea creatures; it's about ensuring a sustainable future for ourselves. The tide of plastic can be reversed, but only if we act together—starting today.
paragraphs.zh: 海洋覆盖了地球表面的71%，并产生了我们呼吸的超过一半的氧气。保护海洋免受塑料污染不仅仅是拯救可爱的海洋生物；而是确保我们自己可持续的未来。塑料潮可以被逆转，但只有我们从今天开始共同努力才能实现。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Plastic pollution is a serious problem that affects marine life and humans, but solutions are possible.
Microplastics are more dangerous than larger plastic items.
Technology can solve plastic pollution easily.
Only governments should take action to reduce plastic waste.
questions.explain: 文章主旨是塑料污染对海洋和人类的影响以及解决方案。选项A全面概括了问题与希望。选项B只是部分细节，选项C过度乐观，选项D忽略了个人责任。

questions.q: According to the passage, how do microplastics enter the human body?
options: Through the food chain, especially by eating fish.
By breathing air in coastal areas.
questions.explain: 文章第四段提到微塑料被小鱼吃掉后，化学物质沿食物链上升，最终出现在人类的餐盘上。选项B正确，其他选项未提及或不符合原文。

questions.q: What does the word "persist" in paragraph 2 most likely mean?
questions.explain: 根据上下文，塑料在环境中存在数百年，"persist"意为"持续存在"，与选项B相符。选项A（快速消失）相反，选项C（变成别的东西）是分解过程，不是persist的含义。

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a solution to plastic pollution?
options: Sinking plastic waste into the deep ocean.
questions.explain: 文章第五、六、七段分别提到了禁令、细菌技术和教育，但未提及将塑料沉入深海。选项C是错误做法，且文中未提，因此是答案。
